title: Hybrids and Router Setups
concept: Router architectures optimize operations by sending simple tasks to cheap local models and complex queries to larger models. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: The Path to AI Agents.'

exercises.prompt: What is the main idea behind a hybrid router setup in AI?

options.text: Using only one giant model for everything
Directing prompts to either small or large models based on difficulty
Replacing all AI models with standard database searches

exercises.explain: A router setup dynamically switches between different models depending on what the user asks, optimizing both speed and cost.

cards.title: Don't Overpay for Simple Tasks
cards.body: Imagine using a supercomputer just to calculate 2 + 2. It's a waste of energy and money! How do we make sure we only use big, expensive AI models when we absolutely need to? We use a smart router!

cards.title: What is a Router?
cards.body: A Router Setup is an architecture where a lightweight coordinator model inspects incoming prompts. It directs simple, routine tasks to a small, inexpensive local model, while sending complex, multi-step queries to a powerful, expensive cloud model.

cards.title: The Mailroom Clerk
cards.body: Think of a router like a mailroom clerk. If a package is just a simple letter, the clerk stamps it and sends it off immediately. But if the package is a complex international shipment with legal forms, the clerk forwards it to a senior logistics manager. The clerk saves the manager's time for the hard stuff!

cards.title: Routing in Action
cards.body: If you ask a smart assistant 'What is 5 + 5?', the router immediately sends it to a tiny local model (fast & free). If you ask 'Write a Python script to analyze stock market trends,' the router sends it to a giant frontier model in the cloud. You get the best of both worlds!

cards.title: The One-Model Myth
cards.body: People think you must choose between a cheap, fast model and a smart, expensive one. In reality, router setups let you use both dynamically, keeping overall costs extremely low while maintaining high-quality answers.

cards.title: Traffic Control
cards.body: Let's compare how tasks flow depending on their complexity in a router setup.

right.text: Routed to a large, smart cloud model. Higher cost, deep reasoning.

left.text: Sent to a small, fast local model. Low cost, instant reply.

exercises.prompt: In a router setup, where would a simple spelling correction task most likely be sent?
exercises.explain: Local models are fast and cheap, making them perfect for basic requests, while advanced models handle the hard stuff.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Using a router setup helps reduce the overall cost of running an AI application.
exercises.explain: Routing saves money by only sending complex prompts to the expensive models, while local models handle the rest.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of this architecture.
exercises.sentence: A smart AI system uses a [blank] to inspect prompts and forward them to the correct model.
exercises.explain: The router acts as the traffic controller, deciding which model is best suited for the incoming prompt.

exercises.prompt: Which task is best routed to a cheap, local model instead of a giant cloud model?

options.text: Analyzing a 50-page legal contract for hidden risks.
Checking a short email draft for spelling mistakes.
Writing a complex simulation codebase from scratch.

exercises.explain: Checking spelling is a simple, rule-based task that doesn't require a giant cloud model, making it perfect for a local router decision. Analyzing complex legal contracts requires a high-reasoning model.

exercises.prompt: How does a router model architecture optimize AI application costs and performance?

options.text: It sends every single prompt to both models at the same time.
It evaluates the prompt complexity and routes it to the most cost-effective model.
It blocks complex queries to save money.
It rewrites the prompt to make it simpler.

exercises.explain: Fantastic! You've mastered how router setups balance speed, cost, and intelligence. Next up: The Path to AI Agents.
